report_name: AI-Powered Attacks on Maritime Industry Weaponize Flaws in Under 48 Hours

threat_actors.name: AI-Powered Maritime Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: A class of threat actors defined by their use of Artificial Intelligence to accelerate and enhance cyberattacks against the global maritime industry. This includes rapidly weaponizing vulnerabilities and crafting sophisticated, native-language phishing emails.

attack_patterns.name: Vulnerability Scanning
attack_patterns.description: AI tools are used to continuously scan the internet for vulnerable maritime IT and OT systems, identifying unpatched software or misconfigured devices much faster than manual methods.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: Generative AI is used to create highly personalized, context-aware, and native-language phishing emails to effectively target diverse, multinational maritime crews.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors rapidly exploit newly disclosed vulnerabilities in public-facing maritime IT and OT systems, with weaponization occurring in under 48 hours due to AI-driven exploit development.

attack_patterns.name: Denial of View
attack_patterns.description: An attack cited in the report caused a total loss of connectivity and control over ship-to-shore VOIP services, inhibiting the operator's view and control, which constitutes a safety and operational incident.

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: AI models are used to analyze disclosed vulnerability details and rapidly generate proof-of-concept exploit code, which is the primary driver for the shrinking vulnerability weaponization window.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: Used by attackers to accelerate the entire attack lifecycle, including reconnaissance (vulnerability scanning), exploit development, and creating highly effective social engineering campaigns (phishing). The report distinguishes between generative AI (as a tool) and agentic AI (as an autonomous actor).

identities.name: Maritime Industry
identities.description: The global maritime industry, including ships, ports, offshore platforms, and logistics, targeted by AI-accelerated cyberattacks.
